what_it_does: It is a contained scratch exploration of how spatial dimension relates to the recursive-depth exponent k in the seed n^(1-k)=2π. It lays out candidate interpretations (D1-D4), applies a kill-condition to discriminate them, records returns and verdicts, synthesizes Tim's resolution that recursion is dimension and 'project is the math', and runs two adversarial refutation passes, all explicitly tagged as AI-generated and unconfirmed.
observations: Explicitly labeled AI-generated, TIM-UNCONFIRMED, DO NOT PROMOTE.
Uses a kill-condition to decide whether Tim's 2π means measure-of-the-circle or one full turn.
Four candidate directions (D1-D4) are held level and told to break themselves, not to sell.
D1 is declared dead; D2 is kept for circle/square/both but rejected as a dimension bridge; C claims k and d are the same exponent; D corpus mine finds φ absent from Tim's words.
Includes Tim verbatim resolution (2026-06-21): recursion is dimension, and 'project is the math'.
Includes two adversarial refutation passes with verdict 'survives-as-candidacy' plus identified cracks.
Repeatedly self-flags cube-error risks, AI glosses, and over-reads.
No code symbols; the symbols list is explicitly empty.
standouts: The kill-condition method: D1 dies because Tim's 2π is 'one full turn', never a measure.
Tim's verbatim synthesis: 'each recursion is another dimension' and 'project is the math'.
Refutation passes conclude the synthesis is a consistent extension of Tim's formalism, not a contradiction, but the recursion⇔spatial-dimension bridge is unwritten.
Strong self-caution against promoting any of this into the UNIFICATION-MAP without Tim's confirmation.
conventions: Heavy provenance tagging (T1/T2/T3, exploration/unconfirmed, kill/support verdicts).
Breaks typical design-doc style by embedding adversarial self-refutation and explicit bias notes.
concerns: Content is AI-generated and explicitly not confirmed by Tim; risk of self-fulfilling interpretation.
Some claims (e.g., k=d same exponent) are stated with high confidence despite the exploration tag.
Circular grounding is noted internally for 'centre = other side of corner'.
The detailed synthesis could be mistaken for findings despite the DO NOT PROMOTE banner.
notes: A self-contained, adversarial exploration log testing whether dimension equals recursive depth in Tim's math, concluding it survives as an unconfirmed extension with build implications.
questions: What is the exact formal definition of 'dimension' in Tim's system—spatial axes, exponent, or recursive depth?
How does a continuous or fractal dimension parameter map to the instrument's UI if dimension is not integer?
Which parts of the synthesis has Tim explicitly confirmed versus still wants disproved?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the stated scratch purpose and goes further: it becomes a structured falsification log with synthesis, refutation passes, and build implications.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to stress-test an AI-generated bridge between dimension and recursive depth using Tim's own words and formal math, while rigorously preventing premature promotion of unconfirmed math into the canonical base.
novelty: It behaves like an adversarial scientific notebook embedded in a design file, complete with kill conditions, bias confessions, explicit retractions, and date-stamped refutation passes.
contribution: A worked methodology for holding candidate interpretations level, applying kill conditions, recording verdicts, and synthesizing an unconfirmed extension with explicit cracks and build implications; also a candidate dimension↔k bridge awaiting Tim's confirmation.
summary_for_embedding: An AI-generated, Tim-unconfirmed exploration log that tests candidate bridges between spatial dimension and recursive depth k in n^(1-k)=2π, records kill-condition verdicts, Tim's resolution that recursion is dimension and projection is the math, and two adversarial refutation passes.
intention_for_embedding: To contain and stress-test an AI-inferred dimension↔k bridge before any part of it is promoted into the canonical UNIFICATION-MAP.
